rules.name: Role Contrast
preconditions: Display, body, and meta content roles are identified before styling.
rules.intent: Create semantic hierarchy through distinct display, body, and meta roles rather than size alone.
constraints: Use display, body, and meta roles.
Keep at least a 1.25 scale between adjacent display roles.
responsiveBehavior: Reduce display scale without collapsing semantic role contrast.
accessibility: Heading semantics must follow document structure independently of visual size.
antiPatterns: Size-only hierarchy with indistinguishable weight, spacing, and semantics.
verification: Inspect heading semantics and computed role contrast, including the 1.25 display-role scale and 45–75ch measure.

rules.name: Editorial Product Voice
preconditions: A narrative or product evidence sequence is present.
rules.intent: Let sourced content and product evidence determine the reading rhythm.
constraints: Keep evidence labels quieter than claims.
Pair product facts with the heading they qualify.
responsiveBehavior: Shorten line length before reducing body size.
accessibility: Do not encode evidence status through italics or color alone.
antiPatterns: Decorative pull quotes without content value.
verification: Confirm narrative order, readable measure, and explicit evidence labels at mobile and desktop widths.

rules.name: Dense Workspace Roles
preconditions: Repeated rows, technical output, or work-object metadata are primary.
rules.intent: Keep dense operational content scannable through stable functional text roles.
constraints: Use tabular numerals where comparisons require them.
Keep action labels distinct from metadata.
responsiveBehavior: Preserve labels when columns become list-detail groups.
accessibility: Maintain readable text size and visible focus labels in compact layouts.
antiPatterns: Shrinking all text to fit a wide table.
verification: Scan repeated rows for stable alignment, role distinction, and readable zoom behavior.
